title: Pyranometer and method of detecting a soiling on a dome in a pyranometer
abstract: The present invention relates to a pyranometer and to a method of detecting a soiling on a dome (1) in a pyranometer (100). According to an aspect, the pyranometer (100) comprises a dome (1) enclosing a cavity (10), at least one light emitting source (5) arranged such that a light exterior to the dome (1) does not directly impinge on the at least one light emitting source, a diffusor (4); wherein the at least one light emitting source (5) is configured to emit a light substantially directed to a portion of the diffusor (4), and wherein the diffusor (4) is configured to diffuse the light emitted from the at least one light emitting source (5) on the dome (1); and one or more first light detecting sensors (6a) arranged in the cavity (10) and configured to measure an intensity of the light reflected from the dome (1) and impinging on the one or more first light detecting sensors (6a).
claims:
Claims 1 . A pyranometer (100) comprising: a dome (1 ) enclosing a cavity (10); at least one light emitting source (5) arranged such that light exterior to the dome (1 ) does not directly impinge on the at least one light emitting source (5); a diffusor (4); a solar radiation sensor (7) being arranged to detect light exterior to the dome (1 ) at least partly impinging on the solar radiation sensor (7) through the diffusor (4), wherein the at least one light emitting source (5) is configured to emit light substantially directed to a portion of the diffusor (4), and wherein the diffusor (4) is configured to diffuse the light emitted from the at least one light emitting source (5) on an inner surface (12) of the dome (1 ); and one or more first light detecting sensors (6a) arranged in the cavity (10) and configured to measure an intensity of the light reflected from the dome (1 ) and impinging on the one or more first light detecting sensors (6a) , further comprising: a pyranometer housing (2) comprising a chamber (20), wherein the least one light emitting source (5) is located in the chamber (20), and wherein the chamber (20) contains the solar radiation sensor (7), at least one partition wall (3) arranged to at least partially partition the chamber (20) from the cavity (10) such that the light exterior to the dome (1 ) does not directly impinge on the at least one light emitting source (5) being arranged in the chamber (20); 46 

 wherein the partition wall (3) comprises a through opening (30), and wherein the diffusor (4) is arranged in the through opening (30) of the partition wall (3). 2. The pyranometer (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, comprising a plurality of first light detecting sensors (6a) arranged in the cavity (10), wherein the plurality of first light detecting sensor (6a) are arranged substantially symmetrically. 3. The pyranometer (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the solar radiation sensor (7) includes at least one thermopile and/or at least one photodiode. 4. The pyranometer (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising a control unit (8) operatively connected with the one or more first light detecting sensors (6a), wherein the control unit (8) is configured to compare the intensity of the light measured by the first light detecting sensor(s) (6a) with a respective pre-set light intensity value. 5. The pyranometer (100) according to claim 4, wherein the control unit (8) is configured to emit a soiling presence signal if the control unit (8) determines that the intensity of the light measured by the first light detecting sensor(s) (6a) differs from the pre-set light intensity value(s) by a specified threshold and/or factor. 6. The pyranometer (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, the pyranometer (100) comprising a plurality of first light detecting sensors (6a), wherein 47 

the control unit (8) is further configured to compare the intensities of the light measured by each of the plurality first light detecting sensors (6a), wherein if a difference in the intensities of the light measured by each of the plurality of first light detecting sensors (6a) is detected by the control unit (8), the control unit (8) is configured to emit a first signal, and/or if a difference in the intensities of the light measured by each of the plurality of first light detecting sensors (6a) is not detected by the control unit (8), the control unit (8) is configured to emit a second signal, wherein the second signal is different from the first signal. 7. The pyranometer (100) according to claim 6, wherein if a difference in the intensities of the light measured by the plurality of first light detecting sensors (6a) is detected by the control unit (8), the control unit (8) is configured to determine an azimuthal angle (a) of a point (P) on the dome (1 ) corresponding to a soiling accumulation, wherein the azimuthal angle (a) is determined based on the difference between the intensities of the light measured by the plurality of first light detecting sensors (6a) and the location of the plurality of first light detecting sensors (6a) in the cavity (10). 8. The pyranometer (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising at least one second light detecting sensor (6b) arranged in the chamber (20), wherein the second light detecting sensor (6b) is arranged such that a light exterior to the dome (1 ) does not directly impinge on the second light detecting sensor (6b), and wherein the second light detecting sensor (6b) is arranged so as to detect a light emitted from the light emitting source (5), reflected from the diffusor (4) and impinging on the second light detecting sensors (6b). 48 9. The pyranometer (100) according to claim 8, wherein the control unit (8) is configured to: compare the intensity of the light measured by the one or more first light detecting sensors (6a) and the intensity of the light measured by the second light detecting sensor (6b), and modify the intensity of the light measured by the one or more first light detecting sensors (6a) if a difference between the intensity of the light measured by the one or more first light detecting sensors (6a) and the intensity of the light measured by the second light detecting sensor (6b) is detected by the control unit (8). 10. The pyranometer (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one light emitting source (5) is configured to emit a light flash, and wherein the control unit (8) is configured to compare a light flash intensity measured by the one or more first light detecting sensors (6a) and the light flash intensity measured by the solar radiation sensor (7). 1 1 . Method of detecting a soiling on a dome (1 ) in a pyranometer (100) according to any one of claims 1 to 10, the method comprising: emitting light substantially directed to a portion of a diffusor (4) by at least one light emitting source (5), wherein the at least one light emitting source (5) is arranged in the pyranometer (100) such that a light exterior to a dome (1 ) of the pyranometer (100) does not directly impinge on the at least one light emitting source (5); and measuring an intensity of the light diffused by the diffusor (4) and reflected from the dome (1 ) by one or more first light detecting sensors (6a) arranged in a cavity (10) enclosed by the dome (1 ) of the pyranometer. 12. Method of detecting a soiling on a dome in a pyranometer (100) according to claim 11 , the method further comprising: comparing the intensity of the light measured by the first light detecting sensor(s) (6a) with a respective pre-set light intensity value; and emitting a soiling presence signal if the intensity of the light measured by the first light detecting sensor(s) (6a) differs from the pre-set light intensity value(s) by a specified threshold and/or factor. 13. Method of detecting a soiling on a dome in a pyranometer (100) according to claim 12, wherein the pyranometer (100) comprises a plurality of first light detecting sensors (6a), the method further comprising: comparing the intensities of the light measured by each of the plurality of first light detecting sensors (6a); and emitting a first signal if a difference in the intensities of the light measured by each of the plurality of first light detecting sensors (6a) is detected, and/or emitting a second signal if a difference in the intensities of the light measured by each of the plurality of first light detecting sensors (6a) is not detected, wherein the second signal being different from the first signal; wherein when a difference in the intensities of the light measured by the plurality of first light detecting sensors (6a) is detected, determining an azimuthal angle (a) of a point (P) on the dome (1 ) corresponding to a soiling accumulation, wherein the azimuthal angle (a) is determined on the difference between the intensity of the light measured by each of the plurality of first light detecting sensors (6a) and the location of the plurality of first light detecting sensors (6a) in the chamber (10). 14. Method of detecting a soiling on a dome in a pyranometer (100) according to any one of claims 1 1 to 13, further comprising: emitting a light substantially directed to a portion of the diffusor (4); and measuring an intensity of the light reflected from the portion of the diffusor (4) by a second light detecting sensor (6b) arranged such that a light exterior to the dome (1 ) does not directly impinge on the second light detecting sensor (6b); further comprising: comparing the intensity of the light measured by the one or more first light detecting sensors (6a) and the intensity of the light measured by the second light detecting sensor (6b); and modifying the intensity of the light measured by the one or more first light detecting sensors (6a) if a difference between the intensity of the light measured by the first light detecting sensor (6a) and the intensity of the light measured by the second light detecting sensor (6b) is detected.
